channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Explorers discover wreckage of cargo ship that sank in Lake Superior storm more than 130 years ago
item.description: Explorers have discovered the sunken wreckage of one of the first steel cargo ships to travel the Great Lakes.
item.dc:creator: Todd Richmond, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Still using an iPad as a Home Hub? Bad news – Apple is about to end support for it
item.description: If you've been putting off upgrading to the new HomeKit architecture, you'll soon be forced to take the plunge.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Future PlayStation games could have AI-powered characters, if this leaked prototype of Aloy is anything to go by
item.description: AI-powered characters could feature in future PlayStation games, and the Horizon series could be the testbed with an AI Aloy.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: How to deliver more secure capabilities to the warfighter, faster
item.description: Rethinking the delivery process for new capabilities is essential to put cybersecurity and the warfighter front and center. The post How to deliver more secure capabilities to the warfighter, faster first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Daisy Thornton, Federal News Network

item.title: Hey Apple! When you’re in a hole, stop digging
item.description: Soon after the non-appearance of the most important Apple Intelligence feature, Apple now finds itself in danger of digging an even deeper hole by promising iOS 19 will be the most significant upgrade in years. The problem: it again runs the risk of promising too much and not delivering enough.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Observe links end-user experience with back-end troubleshooting
item.description: Observe has bolstered its observability platform with frontend monitoring capabilities that it says will enable developers and IT teams to gain visibility into application performance on end-user browsers and mobile applications.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Musk having ‘great difficulty’ running businesses amid DOGE obligations, Tesla stock slide
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk admitted he is having “great difficulty” running his various businesses as he juggles an advisory role in the Trump administration and leads the Department of Government Efficiency's (DOGE) efforts to overhaul the federal workforce, all while Tesla stocks fall.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Ibiza automatiza la detección de alquileres turísticos ilegales con inteligencia de datos
item.description: El Govern y el Consell Insular de Ibiza han anunciado un nuevo paso en la lucha contra el alquiler turístico ilegal con la automatización de su detección.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Trump says he’s buying ‘brand new’ Tesla to support Musk
item.description: President Trump said he would be buying a “brand new” Tesla to support his senior adviser Elon Musk, as the stock market shows shares of the electric car company tumbling.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: DOGE deploys AI chatbot at GSA amid workforce reductions
item.description: The Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), backed by Elon Musk, has expanded the deployment of its custom AI-powered chatbot, GSAi, to approximately 1,500 government workers at the General Services Administration.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Word for Microsoft 365 cheat sheet
item.description: Word has always been the workhorse app of the Microsoft Office suite. Nearly everyone who uses Office ends up using Word at some point, whether it be for writing memos, typing up agendas, creating reports, crafting business correspondence or any of a thousand other uses.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Shock, horror – I’m not going to argue with Microsoft’s latest bit of nagging in Windows 11, as this pop-up is justified
item.description: Windows 11 is getting yet another pop-up, but I’ll give this particular bit of nagging a pass as it could be useful.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Creepy humanoid robot moves using 1,000 eerie artificial muscles
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says Clone Robotics’ Protoclone mimics humans with 1,000 muscles – too human for comfort.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 3 of the best LLM integration tools for R
item.description: When we first looked at this space in late 2023, many generative AI R packages focused largely on OpenAI and ChatGPT-like functionality or coding add-ins. Today, the landscape includes packages that natively support more LLM suppliers—including models run locally on your own computer.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Cuatro pasos para desmentir el mito de la resistencia al cambio
item.description: Ya sea automatizando procesos, reorganizando equipos o adaptándose a las necesidades cambiantes de los clientes, los ejecutivos saben que es imprescindible que todos acepten el cambio para crecer.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Tesla stock plummets, but its sky-high valuation persists. Here’s why
item.description: Tesla’s stock has dropped by nearly half in three months. Even so, investors are still debating whether Elon Musk’s electric-vehicle maker remains overpriced.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Elon Musk claims X being targeted in 'massive cyberattack' as service goes down
item.description: Hours after a series of outages Monday that left X unavailable to thousands of users, Elon Musk claimed that the social media platform was being targeted in a “massive cyberattack.” “We get attacked every day, but this was done with a lot of resources," Musk claimed in a post.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Nissan is testing driverless vehicles in city streets
item.description: The van makes its way slowly but surely through the city streets, braking gently when a car swerves into its lane. But its steering wheel is turning on its own, and there’s no one in the driver’s seat. The driverless technology from Nissan Motor Corp.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: ServiceNow to pay $2.85B for Moveworks’ AI tools
item.description: ServiceNow announced plans to purchase Moveworks, its front-end AI assistant and enterprise search technology for $2.85 billion in cash and stock.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Social media might be making you sweat—literally
item.description: By now everyone knows that scrolling social media isn’t exactly good for you. But did you know it might be making you sweat? Researchers from the psychology department at Durham University tracked the physiological responses of scrolling on social media and found a rather strange side effect.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: AI made its way to vineyards. Here’s how the technology is helping make your wine
item.description: Experts say that the wine industry is proof that businesses can integrate the technology efficiently.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Mark Kelly fires back at Musk over 'traitor' accusation
item.description: Sen. Mark Kelly (D-Ariz.) hit back at tech billionaire Elon Musk after he called Kelly a "traitor" for visiting Ukraine and demanding the United States stand with Ukrainians in their ongoing war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, The Hill

item.title: Trade groups urge the administration to maintain basic research funding
item.description: "The Trump administration's efforts to reduce the size of the federal government have resulted in decreased funding for biomedical research," said Sandra Babosu The post Trade groups urge the administration to maintain basic research funding first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: I tried out a bunch of the AI assistants. Here’s what you need to know about each one
item.description: Does it feel to you like there are way too many AI assistants to keep track of? Between ChatGPT, Microsoft Copilot, Google Gemini, Anthropic Claude, DeepSeek, and others, it’s hard to remember what each one excels at—if anything.
item.dc:creator: Jared Newman, Fast Company

item.title: AI slop is suffocating the web, says a new study
item.description: The generative AI revolution shows no sign of slowing as OpenAI recently rolled out its GPT-4.5 model to paying ChatGPT users, while competitors have announced plans to introduce their own latest models—including Anthropic, which unveiled Claude 3.7 Sonnet, its latest language model, late last month
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Climate change, already causing problems on Earth, could soon create a mess for orbiting satellites
item.description: A new study finds that climate change is already causing all sorts of problems on Earth, but soon it will be making a mess in orbit around the planet too.
item.dc:creator: Seth Borenstein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Is that iPhone app spying? Apple’s App Privacy Report reveals all
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson urges you to use Apple’s App Privacy Report to see what your apps are really up to.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: iDEAL Semiconductor CEO decries CHIPS Act loss, warns of tech talent gap
item.description: In his address to Congress last week, US President Donald J. Trump renewed his criticism of the CHIPS Act, the initiative aimed at reshoring the semiconductor industry, calling it “horrible.” He also called for any remaining funds to be returned to taxpayers.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Vibes won’t make your software successful
item.description: I regret to inform you that vibes don’t pay the bills. Specifically, open source vibes. For as long as I’ve followed open source, there has been a tendency for the industry to focus on normative values of open source, i.e.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 10 things developers love about JavaScript – and 10 things they don’t
item.description: It was 30 years ago today, Sgt. JavaScript taught the web to play. Well, not exactly but close enough. The programming language began in 1995 as a project in the depths of the browser maker Netscape.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How genomics and AI could help treat cancer more effectively
item.description: “These discoveries highlight how genetic profiling can play a crucial role in personalizing cancer care,” Ruishan Liu said in a news release.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Got a bank transfer alert text? It might be a scam; here’s what to do
item.description: Kurt the CyberGuy offers tips to safeguard your personal information as scammers become more sophisticated with scam texts and other methods.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UC teams with Bay Area tech company for new lab that hopes to make AI work for artists
item.description: Can a new initiative make peace between human creatives and the technology threatening to displace them?
item.dc:creator: Jenna Ahart, The Mercury News

item.title: New delivery robot can haul 2,200 pounds of your stuff
item.description: WeRide's unmanned electric delivery van, the Robovan W5, has 194 cubic feet of cargo space and can carry payloads up to about 2,205 pounds
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Microsoft previews AI chat template for .NET
item.description: Microsoft is previewing a .NET web app template intended to enable quick development of AI-powered chat applications. Part of an ongoing effort to make AI development with .NET easier to discover and use, the AI Chat Web App template was announced March 6.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Helping DOGE cut waste
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Utah becomes the first state to pass legislation requiring app stores to verify ages
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — Utah on Wednesday became the first state to pass legislation requiring app stores to verify users’ ages and get parental consent for minors to download apps to their devices. The bill headed to the desk of Gov.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, Fox 5
